title: Amritsar Pinjra Pol ka Chhama Barshik Vivarn
gloss: Or the Sixth Annual Report of the Amritsar Cattle Asylum.
notes: Cattle-asylum (pinjrapol) annual report — Jain/Hindu animal-welfare institution reporting; cow/animal protection infrastructure documented.

title: Punjab Sindh Kshetr Rishi Kesh wa Kankhal ki Chhatwin Barshik Report
gloss: The Sixth Annual Report of the Asylum in Rishi Kesh and Kankhal for Sadhus or Hindu fakirs.
publisher: Babu Bhagwan Das, Manager, Rishi Kesh
notes: 287-page report of the Rishikesh/Kankhal sadhu asylum — Hindu renunciate welfare institution accounting.

author: Sant Ram, Pandit
title: Kya Brahman Guru ghar ke Drohi hain
gloss: A pamphlet which defends the Brahmans against the Sikh accusation that they were implicated in the death of Guru Arjan and that of Guru Govindh Singh's sons, etc.
notes: 'Are Brahmans traitors to the Guru's house?' — a Brahman rebuttal of Sikh (Arya/Singh Sabha) accusations over the Gurus' deaths; direct Hindu–Sikh boundary polemic, homogenization-dynamics data point.

author: Shri Nath Chandar
title: Bharat Milap
gloss: Or the story of Bharat's visit to Ram Chandra in the forest after the death of Dashrath, translated from Bengali by S. Prakash Dev of the Lahore Brahmo Samaj.
notes: Ramayana episode translated Bengali→Hindi by a Brahmo Samaj member. Dated Brahmo Samvat 80.

author: Viddiya Ratn, Pandit
title: Punjab Bhugol
gloss: Or a Geography of the Punjab.
notes: '(Designed for educational purposes.)' printed above entry. Geography of the Punjab from the Kanya Mahavidyalaya (the pioneering Arya Samaj girls' school), Jullundur.

author: Dat Ji
title: Shri Kanwal Netra
gloss: A short poem in the praise of Krishna.
notes: Krishna devotional verse in Hindi written in Urdu script.

author: Narayana Swami
title: Anurag Ras
gloss: A collection of 168 dohas or couplets, didactic and devotional.

title: Jain Bhajan Sangrah
gloss: Or a collection of short religious Jain songs.
notes: Serial continues Hindi—Poetry across the Urdu-character subsection (s1–2 above were Hindi in Urdu character; s3 resumes Nagari). Jain devotional songbook from Jaipur. Dual date: 1966 Bikrami / 1910 A.D.
